description:
Beads uses hierarchical IDs like bd-a3f8.1.1 for readable nesting under a parent epic, alongside the hash-based base ID. Balls today uses only flat hex hashes; parent is a field, not part of the name.

Goal: support a human-readable hierarchical suffix on top of the existing hash ID, without breaking forward/backward compat.

Constraints:
- validate_id() in src/task.rs must keep accepting legacy flat IDs (bl-<hex>) forever. New format is bl-<hex>(.[0-9]+)+.
- Task filenames must stay filesystem-safe (dots are fine on Linux/macOS/Windows NTFS).
- The '.' suffix is display/readability — the hash half remains the collision-resistant primitive. If two agents concurrently create bl-a3f8.1 on different branches, existing merge semantics still need to resolve it (may need auto-renumber on merge).
- An older bl client reading a newer file with a hierarchical id must round-trip it via the extra passthrough, not hard-fail.
- bl create --parent X should auto-assign the next .N under X if --hierarchical is set (or becomes default).
- All bl <id> commands (show/claim/review/close/update/drop/dep/link) resolve both forms.

Open questions:
- Do we number by creation order per parent, or by a deterministic hash-derived number?
- On merge conflict where two branches each minted bl-X.3, who wins? Cheapest answer: append a hash disambiguator (bl-X.3-<hex>) and warn.
- How deep is 'deep enough'? Beads caps at 3 levels; we should decide and document.

Not in scope:
- Renaming existing tasks retroactively.
- Changing the hash-based id generator.
title: Hierarchical sub-task IDs (bl-a3f8.1.1) alongside hash IDs
